mtp.trainer_extension_note: t2_mtp.py reads from ledger/views/wcode-r1.jsonl (hardcoded path). Round-2 wrapper pre-writes the r2 frontier-filtered view and installs it at that path (with backup). A --view-path argument to t2_mtp.py would avoid view stomping; that is a one-line trainer extension not applied per new-files-only constraint. Arm is otherwise fully concrete: MTPTrainer exists in t2_mtp.py and is production-validated.

frontier_filter_dryrun.solve_rate_definition: verified_count / (verified_count + failed_count) where verified_count = rows in ledger/episodes.jsonl for that task, failed_count = rows in ledger/control_pool.jsonl for that task. Tasks with rate=0 (all failed, none verified) are excluded by the open lower bound. Tasks with rate=1 (all verified, none failed) are excluded by theta=0.5. Only tasks with rate in (0, 0.5] pass the filter.

calibration_single_source: calibrate.calibration_block + w1_mbpp.elicit_prompt (imported, not reimplemented); r2_arms.brier() is a thin wrapper over calibrate.calibration_block
mtp_arm_status: concrete — t2_mtp.MTPTrainer exists and is production-validated; round-2 wrapper pre-writes the frontier-filtered wcode view to the path t2_mtp reads. One-line extension (--view-path) would cleanly separate r1/r2 views without file overwrite; noted in PR.
sha_convention: eng receipts hash the CRLF rendering; git content LF; same text (per eng-23 receipt note)

launch_interlock.mechanism: module-level _require_gate_token() executes on import; SystemExit(1) if --leo-gate-token is absent or empty. Fires before any dataset build or training delegation.
launch_interlock.verified_by: manual execution of each wrapper without --leo-gate-token; all three exit 1 with the interlock message
